# Evidence Package Report

**Organisation:** Acme Financial Services Ltd
**Assessment Reference:** GAP-2024-001
**Generated:** 2024-12-01
**Generated by:** Marcus Webb, Head of Information Security
**Reviewed by:** Sarah Chen, CISO
**ISO 27001:2022 Reference:** Clause 7.5 (Documented information), Clause 9.1 (Monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation)

---

## 1. Summary Statistics

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Total evidence items registered | 47 |
| Controls with at least one evidence item | 58 of 86 applicable controls (67%) |
| Controls with evidence gaps (no evidence registered) | 28 controls (33%) |
| Generated evidence documents (via generate_evidence_document) | 6 |
| Evidence items requiring update before certification | 4 |
| Active corrective actions linked to evidence gaps | 2 (CAR-001, CAR-002) |

### Evidence Coverage by Theme

| Theme | Applicable Controls | Controls with Evidence | Coverage |
|-------|--------------------|-----------------------|----------|
| Organizational (5.x) | 37 | 22 | 59% |
| People (6.x) | 8 | 6 | 75% |
| Physical (7.x) | 7 (applicable) | 4 | 57% |
| Technological (8.x) | 34 | 26 | 76% |
| **Total** | **86** | **58** | **67%** |

> **Assessment:** Evidence coverage of 67% is below the level expected for an organisation preparing for certification audit within three months. The 28 controls with no evidence include several that are Critical priority (5.10, 5.23, 5.35, 8.9, 8.25). The remediation roadmap (GAP-2024-001-RR) targets resolution of these gaps. Evidence generation should be prioritised alongside control implementation — it is insufficient to implement a control; evidence of implementation must be captured and registered.

### Evidence Notes

**EVD-001 (AWS IAM Access Review):** This is the emergency access review completed on 2024-10-05 in response to AUDIT-2024-03 Finding F-001. It is registered as active evidence for 5.15 and 5.18, but note that it was a one-off emergency review, not a recurring quarterly review process. Evidence for the ongoing quarterly process will be generated in Q1 2025 under the automated Jira workflow (see CAR-001 Action 1).

**EVD-002 (Penetration Test Report):** The August 2024 penetration test covered the payment API only. A broader penetration test covering all production systems should be scheduled for Q1 2025. This evidence will be dated (>6 months) at the time of the certification audit.

**EVD-003 (Training Completion Report):** Records 67% completion. This evidence demonstrates a gap, not full compliance. Updated evidence reflecting 100% completion is required before the certification audit (target: 2024-12-31).

**EVD-007 (MFA Screenshot):** Screenshot shows 98% MFA adoption. An updated screenshot demonstrating 100% adoption (post CAR-003 closure) is required before the certification audit.

**EVD-008 (Tenable.io Scan Report 2024-08-01):** This is the scan that identified the 14 Critical/High findings addressed in CAR-002. The scan should be considered historical evidence of the gap. Current evidence of the resolved state should be the most recent Tenable.io report (post-CAR-002 closure). A current scan report should be registered as EVD-008b.

## 3. Evidence Gaps

The following 28 controls have no registered evidence in the ISMS evidence register as at 2024-12-01. These gaps must be addressed before the certification audit.

| Control ID | Control Name | Priority | Gap Description | Responsible | Target |
|-----------|--------------|----------|-----------------|-------------|--------|
| 5.2 | Information security roles and responsibilities | High | RACI matrix incomplete; ISMS process register missing backup owners for 8 processes. Evidence partially available but not ISMS-registered. | Sarah Chen | 2024-11-01 (Action 2 of CAR-001 — COMPLETE; evidence registration pending) |
| 5.7 | Threat intelligence | Medium | No threat intelligence feed or service in place. No evidence of threat intelligence inputs to risk assessment. | Marcus Webb | 2025-01-31 |
| 5.8 | Information security in project management | Medium | No documented procedure for information security in project management. No evidence of security requirements reviewed in recent project initiations. | Marcus Webb | 2025-01-31 |
| 5.9 | Inventory of information and other associated assets | High | Asset inventory partially complete in Confluence but not formally maintained or ISMS-controlled. No version history or review date. | J. Patel | 2024-12-31 |
| 5.10 | Acceptable use of information and other associated assets | **CRITICAL** | Acceptable Use Policy does not exist. No evidence possible until policy is created and acknowledged by staff. Policy creation is Phase 1 of remediation roadmap (GAP-2024-001-RR, action 1.1). | L. Okonkwo | 2024-12-08 |
| 5.14 | Information transfer | Medium | No information transfer policy documented. Some evidence exists in supplier agreements but not registered. | Marcus Webb | 2025-01-15 |
| 5.16 | Identity management | High | Identity management procedure not yet documented (in progress, Phase 2 of roadmap). No formal identity register. | J. Patel | 2025-01-12 |
| 5.23 | Information security for use of cloud services | **CRITICAL** | Cloud Security Policy does not exist. Phase 1 roadmap action (1.4) targets 2025-01-05. No evidence possible until policy created. | Marcus Webb | 2025-01-05 |
| 5.35 | Independent review of information security | **CRITICAL** | No independent review programme established. AUDIT-2024-03 is the first internal audit; it must be formally registered as part of the audit programme. Internal audit programme document required. | Sarah Chen | 2025-01-31 |
| 5.36 | Compliance with policies rules and standards | Medium | No compliance checking procedure documented. No evidence of systematic compliance monitoring. | Marcus Webb | 2025-02-28 |
| 5.37 | Documented operating procedures | Medium | Operating procedures exist for some processes (incident handling, BCP) but many processes lack ISMS-controlled documentation. | J. Patel | 2025-02-28 |
| 6.4 | Disciplinary process | Medium | Disciplinary process for information security violations not documented in ISMS. HR policy references disciplinary action generally but no InfoSec-specific document. | L. Okonkwo | 2025-01-31 |
| 8.1 | User endpoint devices | High | Endpoint management policy not documented. MDM (Intune) configuration screenshots not registered. BYOD scope not formally defined in ISMS. | J. Patel | 2024-12-31 |
| 8.2 | Privileged access rights | High | Privileged Access Procedure not yet documented (planned Phase 2 of roadmap). No evidence of PAM tooling or JIT access process. | J. Patel | 2025-02-02 |
| 8.6 | Capacity management | Medium | No capacity management process documented. AWS Auto Scaling configuration not registered as evidence. CloudWatch capacity alarms not linked to a formal procedure. | J. Patel | 2025-01-31 |
| 8.8 | Management of technical vulnerabilities | High | Note: CAR-002 in progress — Vulnerability Management Procedure (PROC-VM-001) approved 2024-11-15. Evidence to be registered: PROC-VM-001, Tenable.io → Jira integration screenshot, current scan report. Registration pending. | Marcus Webb | 2024-12-01 (registration) |
| 8.9 | Configuration management | **CRITICAL** | No configuration baseline document. AWS Config rules defined but baseline document not created or approved. Phase 1 roadmap action (1.2) targets 2024-12-15. | J. Patel | 2024-12-15 |
| 8.10 | Information deletion | Medium | No data deletion procedure documented. No evidence of controlled deletion of customer data at end of retention period. | J. Patel | 2025-01-31 |
| 8.11 | Data masking | Medium | No data masking procedure. No evidence that PAN data is masked in non-production environments. | Lead Engineer | 2025-02-28 |
| 8.12 | Data leakage prevention | Medium | No DLP policy or tooling. No evidence of controls preventing unauthorised data exfiltration. | Marcus Webb | 2025-02-28 |
| 8.16 | Monitoring activities | Medium | CloudWatch configured but no formal monitoring procedure documented. No evidence of periodic monitoring review. | Marcus Webb | 2025-01-15 |
| 8.18 | Use of privileged utility programs | Medium | No policy restricting use of privileged utility programs. No evidence of controls in place. | J. Patel | 2025-02-28 |

| 8.28 | Secure coding | High | No secure coding standards document. No PR template with security checklist. Phase 3 roadmap action (3.4) targets 2025-04-13. | Lead Engineer | 2025-04-13 |

## 4. Generated Evidence Documents

The following evidence documents were generated using the `generate_evidence_document` tool in iso27001-mcp, providing templated attestations, reports, and records to supplement organisationally-created evidence.

| ID | Template | Title | Status | Generated | Approved by |
|----|----------|-------|--------|-----------|-------------|
| DOC-001 | access_review_attestation | Q3 2024 Emergency Access Review Sign-Off | approved | 2024-10-06 | Sarah Chen |
| DOC-002 | risk_treatment_sign_off | Risk Treatment Plan — Customer Database Encryption | approved | 2024-10-15 | Sarah Chen |
| DOC-003 | supplier_security_questionnaire | AWS (Amazon Web Services) Annual Security Review 2024 | approved | 2024-11-01 | Marcus Webb |
| DOC-004 | training_acknowledgement | Security Awareness Training Programme — Annual Acknowledgement | draft | 2024-11-20 | Pending |
| DOC-005 | incident_post_mortem | Payment Gateway Latency Incident — October 2024 | approved | 2024-11-10 | Marcus Webb |
| DOC-006 | bcp_test_report | Business Continuity Plan Test — AWS Region Failover Q2 2024 | approved | 2024-07-05 | J. Patel |

### Generated Document Notes

**DOC-001 (Emergency Access Review Sign-Off):** Provides the formal attestation for the emergency access review conducted following AUDIT-2024-03 F-001. Linked to CAR-001. This document, combined with the Jira automation configuration (CAR-001 Action 1), provides the evidence package for control 5.18.

**DOC-002 (Risk Treatment Sign-Off):** Sign-off attestation for the decision to implement AWS KMS encryption for all RDS databases and S3 buckets containing customer data. Links to RISK-009 in the risk register and provides evidence for 8.24 (cryptography).

**DOC-003 (AWS Supplier Security Questionnaire):** Annual security review of AWS as a primary infrastructure supplier. Documents review of AWS ISO 27001 certificate (current), SOC 2 Type II report, AWS Shared Responsibility Model, and specific configuration review of eu-west-1 region. Linked to controls 5.19, 5.20, 5.22.

**DOC-004 (Training Acknowledgement — DRAFT):** Template for staff acknowledgement of security awareness training completion. Currently in draft pending finalisation of training delivery format for 2025. Must be approved and distributed before certification audit.

**DOC-005 (Payment Gateway Latency Incident PIR):** Post-incident review report for a P3 payment gateway latency incident in October 2024. Not a security breach; classified as availability incident. Provides evidence for 5.27 (learning from incidents) and the incident management process (5.26).

**DOC-006 (BCP Test Report):** Report from the AWS region failover test conducted in Q2 2024. Demonstrates that Acme's payment processing can failover to a secondary configuration within the defined RTO. Provides evidence for 5.30 (ICT readiness for business continuity).

---

## 5. Recommended Immediate Actions (Top 5 Before Certification)

The following 5 evidence gaps represent the highest priority items to close before the certification audit. Failure to address these is likely to result in major non-conformity findings at the Stage 2 certification audit.

### Priority 1 — Register Vulnerability Management Evidence (EVD-008b, PROC-VM-001)

**Controls:** 8.8
**Why critical:** CAR-002 has been closed and the Vulnerability Management Procedure (PROC-VM-001) is approved. However, the procedure and current scan report have not yet been formally registered as ISMS evidence. This must be done immediately — it is a simple administrative action.
**Action:** J. Patel and Marcus Webb to register PROC-VM-001 and most recent Tenable.io report as evidence items by 2024-12-05.
**Effort:** Low (< 1 hour)

### Priority 2 — Create and Register Acceptable Use Policy Evidence (5.10)

**Controls:** 5.10
**Why critical:** The Acceptable Use Policy does not exist (GAP-2024-001 Critical gap). This is a fundamental document that certification auditors will expect to see. Policy creation is in progress (target 2024-12-08).
**Action:** Finalise and approve Acceptable Use Policy; obtain staff acknowledgements; register policy and acknowledgement records as evidence. Owner: L. Okonkwo.
**Effort:** Low (2 days for policy; ongoing for acknowledgements)

### Priority 3 — Register CAR-001 Closure Evidence

**Controls:** 5.15, 5.18
**Why critical:** CAR-001 (major non-conformity from AUDIT-2024-03) must be fully closed before the certification audit. Actions 1 and 2 are complete; Actions 3 and 4 are in progress. All closure evidence must be registered in ISMS.
**Action:** On closure of Actions 3 and 4, J. Patel and Sarah Chen to register full CAR-001 evidence package: Jira automation screenshot, escalation test, PROC-ARR-001, Q1 2025 access review initiation.
**Effort:** Low to Medium (evidence registration for already-completed work)

### Priority 4 — Configuration Baseline Document and AWS Config Evidence

**Controls:** 8.9
**Why critical:** Configuration management is a Critical gap (not implemented). AWS Config rules exist but no baseline document. Phase 1 roadmap action targets 2024-12-15.
**Action:** J. Patel to create Configuration Management Baseline document; register document and AWS Config conformance pack screenshot as evidence.
**Effort:** Medium (8 person-days — see roadmap action 1.2)

### Priority 5 — Internal Audit Programme Document

**Controls:** 5.35, Clause 9.2
**Why critical:** An internal audit programme is a mandatory clause requirement (9.2) and a Critical gap (5.35 not implemented). AUDIT-2024-03 is the first audit in the programme but has not been formally registered as such within an audit programme document.
**Action:** Sarah Chen to create Internal Audit Programme document, registering AUDIT-2024-03 as Audit 1 and scheduling subsequent audits.
**Effort:** Low to Medium (4 person-days)
